{{Short description|Men's association football team}}
{{About|the men's team|the women's team|Grenada women's national football team}}
{{Use dmy dates|date=October 2023}}
{{Infobox national football team
| Name                  = Grenada
| Badge                 = Flag of Grenada.svg
| Nickname              = ''The Spice Boys''
| Association           = [[Grenada Football Association]] (GFA)
| Confederation         = [[CONCACAF]] (North America)
| Sub-confederation     = [[Caribbean Football Union|CFU]] (Caribbean)
| Coach                 = [[Anthony Modeste (Grenadian footballer)|Anthony Modeste]]
| Captain               = [[Saydrel Lewis]]
| Most caps             = [[Cassim Langaigne]] (72)
| Top scorer            = [[Ricky Charles]] (37)
| Home Stadium          = [[Kirani James Athletic Stadium|Kirani James Stadium]]
| FIFA Trigramme        = GRN
| FIFA Rank             = {{FIFA World Rankings|GRN}}
| FIFA max              = 88
| FIFA max date         = July 2009
| FIFA min              = 176
| FIFA min date         = December 2007
| Elo Rank              = {{World Football Elo Ratings|Grenada}}
| Elo max               = 126
| Elo max date          = August 2004
| Elo min               = 186
| Elo min date          = November 2018
| pattern_la1           = _greenborder
| pattern_b1            = _grn23a
| pattern_ra1           = _greenborder
| pattern_sh1           = 
| pattern_so1           = 
| leftarm1              = FF0000
| body1                 = FF0000
| rightarm1             = FF0000
| shorts1               = FF0000
| socks1                = FF0000
| pattern_la2           = _redborder
| pattern_b2            = _grn23h
| pattern_ra2           = _redborder
| pattern_sh2           = 
| pattern_so2           = 
| leftarm2              = 008000
| body2                 = 008000
| rightarm2             = 008000
| shorts2               = 008000
| socks2                = 008000
| First game            = {{fb|GRN|1903}} 2–1 {{fb-rt|British Guiana}}<br>([[Grenada]]; 13 October 1934)
| Largest win           = {{nowrap|{{fb|GRN}} 14–1 {{fb-rt|AIA}}<br>([[St. John's, Antigua and Barbuda]]; 15 April 1998)}}
| Largest loss          = {{fb|CUW}} 10–0 {{fb-rt|GRN}}<br>([[Willemstad]], [[Curaçao]]; 10 September 2018)
| Regional name         =  [[CONCACAF Gold Cup]]
| Regional cup apps     = 3
| Regional cup first    = [[2009 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2009]]
| Regional cup best     = Group stage ([[2009 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2009]], [[2011 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2011]], [[2021 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2021]])
| medaltemplates        =
{{MedalCompetition|[[Caribbean Cup]]}}
{{MedalSilver|[[1989 Caribbean Cup|1989 Barbados]]|Team}}
{{MedalSilver|[[2008 Caribbean Cup|2008 Jamaica]]|Team}}
}}

The '''Grenada national football team'''  represents [[Grenada]] in men's international [[Association football|football]], which is governed by the [[Grenada Football Association]] founded in 1924. It has been an affiliate member of [[FIFA]] since 1978 and an affiliate member of [[CONCACAF]] since 1969. Regionally, it is an affiliate member of [[Caribbean Football Union|CFU]] in the Caribbean Zone.

Grenada has never participated in the [[FIFA World Cup]], but has qualified for the [[CONCACAF Gold Cup]] three times, and has also participated twice in League A and twice in League B of the [[CONCACAF Nations League]]. Regionally, the team finished as runners-up in the [[Caribbean Cup]] twice ([[1989 Caribbean Cup|1989]] and [[2008 Caribbean Cup|2008]]).

==History==
===Beginnings and early tournaments (1934–1989)===
Grenada  played its first international match, on 13 October 1934, against [[British Guiana national football team|British Guiana]], which they defeated 2–1. They played [[British Guiana national football team|British Guiana]] twice, [[Barbados national football team|Barbados]] and [[Saint Kitts and Nevis national football team|St Kitts and Nevis]] before the [[Second World War]] started in 1939, winning all of those contests.

In 1961, Grenada participated in the [[Windward Islands Tournament]], losing their opening game to [[Saint Vincent and the Grenadines national football team|Saint Vincent and the Grenadines]] 6–3, but winning their next two matches against [[Saint Lucia national football team|Saint Lucia]] 4–0 and [[Dominica national football team|Dominica]] 8–1 to win the tournament. Grenada won the tournament again the following year, beating Saint Vincent 2–1 in the final, and for four consecutive years from 1967 to 1970.

Grenada played their first competitive match in the [[1979 CFU Championship|1979 CFU Championship qualifiers]] against [[Trinidad and Tobago national football team|Trinidad and Tobago]], losing 3–1 on aggregate. Grenada entered the [[1981 CONCACAF Championship qualification]] but lost in the preliminary round to [[Guyana national football team|Guyana]] 8–4 over two legs. Their next competitive match came in the [[1985 CFU Championship|1985 CFU Championship qualifiers]], but they lost both matches and failed to progress past the first round. Grenada had more success in the CFU Championship's successor tournament, reaching the [[1989 Caribbean Cup]] final before losing 2–1 to [[Trinidad and Tobago national football team|Trinidad and Tobago]].

===Caribbean Cup and qualifiers (1990–2010)===

Grenada qualified for the [[1990 Caribbean Cup]] but lost again to [[Trinidad and Tobago national football team|Trinidad and Tobago]], this time 5–0 before drawing with [[Jamaica national football team|Jamaica]] 0–0 therefore failing to advance. In the qualifiers for the [[1994 Caribbean Cup]], Grenada was eliminated by Barbados in [[Barbados 4–2 Grenada|an infamous match]] where [[Barbados national football team|Barbados]] purposefully scored an own-goal in order to force overtime and help them to advance, which they did. Grenada qualified to the [[1997 Caribbean Cup]], reaching the semi-finals before losing to [[Saint Kitts and Nevis national football team|Saint Kitts and Nevis]]. Grenada entered the [[1998 FIFA World Cup qualification (CONCACAF)|1998 World Cup qualifiers]] but after getting through the preliminary round by beating [[Guyana national football team|Guyana]] 8–1 on aggregate, they lost 7–1 over two legs to [[Haiti national football team|Haiti]].

In the [[2002 FIFA World Cup qualification (CONCACAF)|2002 World Cup qualifiers]], Grenada was knocked out by [[Barbados national football team|Barbados]] who won with a tight 5–4 aggregate score. They were luckier four years later in the [[2006 FIFA World Cup qualification (CONCACAF)|2006 World Cup qualifiers]], once again eliminating [[Guyana national football team|Guyana]] (8–1 on aggregate) in the first round before falling to the [[United States men's national soccer team|USA]] who edged them 6–2. Grenada would close this decade with a new World Cup disappointment, in the [[2010 FIFA World Cup qualification (CONCACAF)|2010 World Cup qualifiers]], being eliminated by [[Costa Rica national football team|Costa Rica]] 5–2.

===Gold Cup===

Nineteen years after finishing runner-up in the [[1989 Caribbean Cup]], the ''Spice Boyz'' once again reached the final, in the [[2008 Caribbean Cup|2008 edition]], although they lost to the hosts, [[Jamaica national football team|Jamaica]] 2–0. However, this result allowed them to qualify for the [[2009 CONCACAF Gold Cup]] for the first time, however Grenada failed to score a goal in the competition whilst conceding ten times and collecting no points. In the [[2010 Caribbean Cup]], Grenada reached the semi-finals before again being eliminated by Jamaica though their performance earned them qualification for the [[2011 CONCACAF Gold Cup]] but they again failed to capitalise, losing all three group matches and scoring only a single goal.

===2012–present===

Since 2012, Grenada have not managed to progress to the [[Caribbean Cup]] or the [[CONCACAF Gold Cup]]. In the [[2014 FIFA World Cup qualification (CONCACAF)|2014 FIFA World Cup qualifiers]], they finished bottom of their qualifying group despite being the top seed, then in [[2018 FIFA World Cup qualification (CONCACAF)|2018 World Cup qualification]] they were eliminated by [[Haiti national football team|Haiti]] in the third round.

In the [[2019–20 CONCACAF Nations League qualifying]], Grenada suffered its largest ever defeat, losing 10–0 against [[Curaçao national football team|Curaçao]]. They'd eventually qualify for [[2019–20 CONCACAF Nations League B|League B]] before earning promotion to League A for the [[2022–23 CONCACAF Nations League]] competition.

==Results and fixtures==
The following is a list of match results in the last 12 months, as well as any future matches that have been scheduled.

{{legend2|#CCFFCC|Win|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFFFCC|Draw|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFCCCC|Loss|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFFFFF|Fixture|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}

===2025===
{{footballbox collapsible
|round      = [[exhibition match|Friendly]]
|date       = 31 May
|time       = 
|team1      = {{fb-rt|GRN}}
|score      = 2–0
|team2      = {{fb|AIA}}
|report     = https://www.facebook.com/GrenadaFA/posts/pfbid0hHgijyMQx2DbdgsZiuKYgGFB2LfHVs3UkmkfGbNZ6tTLTWEvDuNjNYhHdPKT2mG9l
|goals1     =
*[[Keishean Francois]] {{goal|17}}
*[[Johnathan Williams (footballer)|Johnathan Williams]] {{goal|53}}
|goals2     =
|location   = [[St. George's, Grenada|St. George's]], Grenada
|stadium    = [[Kirani James Athletic Stadium]]
|attendance =
|referee    = 
|result     = W
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|round      = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CONCACAF second round#Group B|2026 FIFA World Cup qualification]]
|date       = 4 June
|time       = {{UTZ|19:00|-4}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|GRN}}
|score      = 6–0
|team2      = {{fb|BAH}}
|report     = https://www.fifa.com/fifaplus/en/match-centre/match/520/288301/288302/400017726
|goals1     =
*[[Jermaine Francis|Francis]] {{goal|15}}
*[[Darius Johnson (footballer)|Johnson]] {{goal|36}}
*[[Keishean Francois|Francois]] {{goal|58}}
*[[Joshua Isaac|Isaac]] {{goal|72}}
*[[Kayden Harrack|Harrack]] {{goal|77}}
*[[Saydrel Lewis|Lewis]] {{goal|90}}
|goals2     =
|location   = [[St. George's, Grenada|St. George's]], Grenada
|stadium    = [[Kirani James Athletic Stadium]]
|attendance = 2,235
|referee    = Shavin Greene ([[Guyana Football Federation|Guyana]])
|result     = W
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|round      = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CONCACAF second round#Group B|2026 FIFA World Cup qualification]]
|date       = 10 June
|time       = {{UTZ|--:--|-4}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|SKN}}
|score      = 2–3
|team2      = {{fb|GRN}}
|report     = https://www.concacaf.com/en/world-cup-qualifying-men/game-details?competition=3a0j0giz3c3ajw9h59evv7lqt&season=excflxj3ypj69gf2wlx7kpudw&match=5334o2sfhrfg93coyos7uc6j8
|goals1     = *[[Tyreece Simpson|Simpson]] {{goal|34}}
*[[Romaine Sawyers|Sawyers]] {{goal|90+4}}
|goals2     =*[[Regan Charles-Cook|Reg. Charles Cook]] {{goal|49}}
*[[Jermaine Francis|Francis]] {{goal|76}}
*[[Darius Johnson (footballer)|Johnson]] {{goal|90+1}}
|location   = [[Basseterre]], [[Saint Kitts and Nevis]]
|stadium    = [[Warner Park Sporting Complex]]
|attendance = 
|referee    = Cristhofer Corado ([[National Football Federation of Guatemala|Guatemala]])
|result     = W
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|round      = [[exhibition match|Friendly]]
|date       = 8 October
|time       = {{UTZ|18:00|-4}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|GRN}}
|score      = 4–1
|team2      = {{fb|VGB}}
|report     = https://www.espn.com/soccer/match/_/gameId/759725/british-virgin-islands-grenada
|goals1     =
|goals2     =
|location   = [[St. George's, Grenada|St. George's]], Grenada
|stadium    = [[Kirani James Athletic Stadium]]
|attendance = 
|referee    = Timothy Derry ([[Trinidad and Tobago Football Association|Trinidad and Tobago]])<ref>{{cite news |title=Grenada vs. British Virgin Islands Match Report – Wednesday October 8, 2025 |url=https://fbref.com/en/matches/7c98fd57/Grenada-British-Virgin-Islands-October-8-2025-Friendlies-M |access-date=22 October 2025 |work=FBREF.com |date=8 October 2025}}</ref>
|result     = W
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|round      = [[Exhibition match|Friendly]]
|date       = 11 October
|time       = {{UTZ|19:30|-4}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|GRN}}
|score      = 2–0
|team2      = {{fb|CUB}} (domestic team)
|report     = https://www.beinsports.com/en-us/soccer/friendlies/grenada-vs-cuba-2025-10-11#OverView
|goals1     =
*[[D'Margio Wright-Phillips|Wright-Phillips]] {{goal|8}}
*[[Shavon John-Brown|John-Brown]] {{goal|42}}
|goals2     =
|stadium    = [[Kirani James Athletic Stadium]]
|location   = [[St. George's, Grenada|St. George's]], Grenada
|attendance =
|referee    = Timothy Derry ([[Trinidad and Tobago Football Association|Trinidad and Tobago]])<ref>{{cite web |title=Granada vs Cuba |url=https://canchallena.lanacion.com.ar/futbol/amistosos-selecciones/granada-cuba-4qvd5vrfosxi3v5nna4g0dq8k/ |publisher=[[La Naciión]] |access-date=21 October 2025 |language=es |date=2025}}</ref>
|result     = W
}}
{{football box collapsible
|round             = [[exhibition match|Friendly]]
|date              = 14 November
|time              = 
|team1             = {{fb-rt|GRN}}
|score             = 4–1
|report            = https://www.facebook.com/share/p/1DGgF4nxM5/?mibextid=wwXIfr
|team2             = {{fb|VIR}}
|goals1            = 
*[[Paris Muirhead|Muirhead]] {{goal|11}}
*[[Regan Charles-Cook|Charles-Cook]] {{goal|27||34}}
*[[Zade Douglas|Douglas]] {{goal|56}}
|goals2            = 
*[[Jannick Liburd|Liburd]] {{goal|71}}
|stadium           = [[Kirani James Athletic Stadium]]
|location          = [[St. George's, Grenada|St. George's]], [[Grenada]]
|attendance        = 
|referee           = 
|result            = W
}}

===2026===
{{Footballbox collapsible
|round      = [[Exhibition game|Friendly]]
|date       = 18 January
|time       = {{UTZ|15:00|-4}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|GRN}}
|score      = 0–1
|team2      = {{fb|JAM}}
|report     = https://www.beinsports.com/en-us/soccer/friendlies/grenada-vs-jamaica-2026-01-18#OverView
|goals1     =
|goals2     = [[Nickalia Fuller|N. Fuller]] {{goal|81}}
|location   = [[St. George's, Grenada|St. George's]], Grenada
|stadium    = [[Kirani James Stadium]]
|attendance = 
|referee    = 
|result     = L
}}
{{football box collapsible
| round      = [[2026 FIFA Series (men's matches)|2026 FIFA Series]]
| date       = 27 March
| time       = {{UTZ|21:00|2}}
| team1      = {{fb-rt|RWA}}
| score      = 4–0
| team2      = {{fb|GRN}}
| report     = https://globalsportsarchive.com/en/soccer/match/2026-03-27/rwanda-vs-grenada/4103613
| goals1     = *[[Leroy-Jacques Mickels]] {{goal|45+2}}
*[[Jojea Kwizera|J. Kwizera]] {{goal|45+6}}
*[[Djihad Bizimana|Bizimana]] {{goal|69}}
*[[Hakim Sahabo|Sahabo]] {{goal|83}}
| goals2     = 
| location   = [[Kigali]], Rwanda
| stadium    = [[Amahoro Stadium]]
| attendance = 
| referee    = [[Haimanot Adane]] ([[Ethiopian Football Federation|Ethiopia]])
| result     = L
}}
{{football box collapsible
| round      = [[2026 FIFA Series (men's matches)|2026 FIFA Series]]
| date       = 30 March
| time       = {{UTZ|16:00|+2}}
| note       = The third-place match and the final of the [[2026 FIFA Series (men's matches)#Rwanda A|Rwanda A section of the 2026 FIFA Series]], originally scheduled for 18:00 and 21:00 local time, respectively, were brought forward to 16:00 and 19:30 local time at the request of the participating teams.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://jalgpall.ee/koondis/1/uudised/selgus-eesti-ja-rwanda-vahelise-fifa-series-turniiri-finaali-manguaeg-n25787|title=Selgus Eesti ja Rwanda vahelise FIFA Series turniiri finaali mänguaeg|trans-title=The kickoff time for the FIFA Series tournament final between Estonia and Rwanda has already been announced|publisher=[[Estonian Football Association]]|date=28 March 2026|language=et}}</ref>
| team1      = {{fb-rt|GRN}}
| score      = 0–3
| team2      = {{fb|KEN}}
| report     = https://www.fifa.com/en/match-centre/match/10017/292887/292888/400021869?date=2026-03-30
| goals1     = 
| goals2     = *[[Austin Odhiambo|A. Odhiambo]] {{goal|13}}
*[[Ryan Ogam|Ogam]] {{goal|18}}
*[[Vijay Valcin|Valcin]] {{goal|81|o.g.}}
| location   = [[Kigali]], Rwanda
| stadium    = [[Amahoro Stadium]]
| attendance = 31,200
| referee    = [[Ahmed Arajiga]] ([[Tanzania Football Federation|Tanzania]])
| result     = L
}}
{{football box collapsible
|round      = [[Exhibition game#International football|Friendly]]
|date       = 16 May
|time       = {{UTZ|18:00|2}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|GRN}}
|score      = 0–5
|team2      = {{fb|IRL}}
|report     = https://www.fai.ie/latest/report-ireland-5-0-grenada-moylan-hat-trick-and-cannon-brace-seal-win/
|goals1     =
|goals2     =
*[[Tom Cannon (footballer)|Cannon]] {{goal|46||56}}
*[[Jack Moylan|Moylan]] {{goal|62||80||84}}
|location   = [[Murcia]], Spain
|stadium    = [[Estadio Nueva Condomina]]
|attendance = 862
|referee    = Seth Galia ([[Gibraltar Football Association|Gibraltar]])
|result     = L
}}

==Coaching staff==

[[File:Tommy-Taylor-2009-07-04.jpg|thumb|200px|[[Tommy Taylor (footballer, born 1951)|Thomas Frederick Taylor]] became the manager of the national football team of Grenada in 2009]]

{| class=wikitable
|-
! Position
! Name
|-
|Technical director
|{{flagicon|GRN}} Alister Fleming
|-
|Head coach
|{{flagicon|GRN}} [[Anthony Modeste (Grenadian footballer)|Anthony Modeste]]
|-
|Assistant coaches
|{{flagicon|GRN}} Ryan Cummings<br>{{flagicon|GRN}} Graeme Constance<br>{{flagicon|GRN}} Jamal Trafford
|-
|Fitness coaches
|{{flagicon|GRN}} Hayden Roberts<br>{{flagicon|GRN}} Luther Jeffords
|-
|Physiotherapists
|{{flagicon|GRN}} James Neil<br>{{flagicon|GRN}} Denzel Daniels<br>{{flagicon|GRN}} Khalil Stevens
|-
|Team doctors
|{{flagicon|GRN}} Lawrence Cooper<br>{{flagicon|GRN}} Andrew Sherman
|-
|Masseur
|{{flagicon|GRN}} Adam Courts
|-
|Osteopath
|{{flagicon|GRN}} Wesley Lewis
|-
|Team nutritionists
|{{flagicon|GRN}} Rebecca Pembroke<br>{{flagicon|GRN}} Graham Murray
|-
|Team cookers
|{{flagicon|GRN}} Timothy Hogg<br>{{flagicon|GRN}} Alvin Marshall<br>{{flagicon|GRN}} Jessica Mitchell<br>{{flagicon|GRN}} LeRoy Hendricks<br>{{flagicon|GRN}} Rashid Goodwin
|-
|Team coordinator
|{{flagicon|GRN}} Marcellus Henshaw
|-
|Technical director
|{{flagicon|GRN}} Gary Hudson
|-
|Head of delegation
|{{flagicon|GRN}} Raymond Bullock
|-
|}

===Coaching history===
{{Div col|colwidth=20em}}
*{{flagicon|FRG}} [[Rudi Gutendorf]] (1976)
*{{flagicon|ARG}} [[Carlos Cavagnaro]] (1986–87)
*{{flagicon|BRA}} [[Carlos Alberto da Luz]] (2000)
*{{flagicon|TRI}} [[Franklyn Simpson]] (2002)
*{{flagicon|GRN}} Alister De Bellotte (2004)
*{{flagicon|GRN}} [[Anthony Modeste (Grenadian footballer)|Anthony Modeste]] (2007–08)*
*{{flagicon|GRN}} [[Norris Wilson]] (2008–09)
*{{flagicon|ENG}} [[Tommy Taylor (footballer born 1951)|Tommy Taylor]] (2009–10)
*{{flagicon|TRI}} [[Franklyn Simpson]] (2010–11)
*{{flagicon|ENG}} [[Mike Adams (football coach)|Mike Adams]] (2011–12)
*{{flagicon|GRN}} Alister De Bellotte (2012)
*{{flagicon|GRN}} [[Clark John]] (2013–14)
*{{flagicon|GRN}} [[Anthony Modeste (Grenadian footballer)|Anthony Modeste]] (2014–15)
*{{flagicon|URU}} [[Jorge Añón]] (2015)
*{{flagicon|GRN}} [[Andrew Munro (footballer)|Andrew Munro]] (2016)
*{{flagicon|GRN}} [[Ashley Folkes]] (2017–2018)
*{{flagicon|GRN}} [[Shalrie Joseph]] (2018–2019)
*{{flagicon|GRN}} [[Andrew Munro (footballer)|Andrew Munro]] (2019–2020)
*{{flagicon|CAN}} [[Michael Findlay (soccer)|Michael Findlay]] (2021–2022)
*{{flagicon|GRN}} [[Anthony Modeste (Grenadian footballer)|Anthony Modeste]] (2022–2023)
*{{flagicon|ENG}} [[Terry Connor]] (2023–2024)
*{{flagicon|GRN}} [[Shalrie Joseph]] (2024)
*{{flagicon|GRN}} [[Anthony Modeste (Grenadian footballer)|Anthony Modeste]] (2025–present)
{{div col end}}
{{small|*Player-coach}}

==Players==
===Current squad===
The following players were called up for the [[Exhibition game#International football|friendly]] match against [[Republic of Ireland national football team|Republic of Ireland]] on 16 May 2026 in [[Murcia]], Spain.<ref>{{cite news |title=The Grenada Football Association (GFA) is proud to announce the official squad for the Senior Men’s National Team ahead of their highly anticipated international friendly match against the Republic of Ireland |url=https://www.facebook.com/photo?fbid=1619689430163566&set=a.519647016834485 |access-date=11 May 2026 |work=Facebook |publisher=Grenada Football Association |date=11 May 2026}}</ref>

''Caps and goals correct as of 16 May 2026, after the match against [[Republic of Ireland national football team|Republic of Ireland]].''

{{nat fs g start}}
{{nat fs g player|no=1|pos=GK|name=Trishwan Thomas|age={{bda|df=y|2003|01|25}}|caps=7|goals=0|club=Grenada Police|clubnat=GRN}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=GK|name=Shaquille Charles|age={{bda|2004|4|16|df=y}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[St. John's SC|St. John's]]|clubnat=GRN}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=2|pos=DF|name=Benjamin Ettienne|age={{bda|2003|3|13|df=y}}|caps=27|goals=0|club=[[Queens Park Rangers SC|Queens Park Rangers]]|clubnat=GRN}}
{{nat fs g player|no=3|pos=DF|name=[[Joshua Lett]]|age={{bda|2004|09|09|df=y}}|caps=3|goals=0|club=[[Sunderland A.F.C.|Sunderland]]|clubnat=ENG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=5|pos=DF|name=[[Kayden Harrack]]|age={{bda|2003|2|28|df=y}}|caps=20|goals=1|club=[[Dagenham & Redbridge F.C.|Dagenham & Redbridge]]|clubnat=ENG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=11|pos=DF|name=[[Shavon John-Brown]]|age={{bda|df=y|1995|4|13}}|caps=48|goals=4|club=[[Spokane Velocity]]|clubnat=USA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=13|pos=DF|name=[[Greg Sandiford]]|age={{birth date and age|2005|05|07|df=yes}}|caps=4|goals=0|club=[[Bedford Town F.C.|Bedford Town]]|clubnat=ENG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=16|pos=DF|name=[[Kane Vincent-Young]]|age={{bda|1996|3|15|df=y}}|caps=8|goals=0|club=[[Colchester United F.C.|Colchester United]]|clubnat=ENG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=21|pos=DF|name=[[Josh Gabriel (footballer)|Josh Gabriel]]|age={{bda|1999|11|30|df=y}}|caps=24|goals=0|club=St. David's|clubnat=GRN}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Jacob Bedeau]]|age={{bda|1999|12|24|df=y}}|caps=4|goals=0|club=[[Notts County F.C.|Notts County]]|clubnat=ENG}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=4|pos=MF|name=Dorrel Pierre|age={{bda|1999|5|5|df=y}}|caps=16|goals=1|club=[[All Saints United F.C.|All Saints United]]|clubnat=ATG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=8|pos=MF|name=[[Myles Hippolyte]]|age={{bda|df=y|1994|11|9}}|caps=11|goals=6|club=[[AFC Wimbledon]]|clubnat=ENG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=10|pos=MF|name=[[Keston Williams]]|age={{bda|df=y|2004|2|22}}|caps=7|goals=1|club=[[Paradise FC International]]|clubnat=GRN}}
{{nat fs g player|no=14|pos=MF|name=Parish Muirhead|age={{birth date and age|2000|12|26|df=yes}}|caps=9|goals=1|club=[[Lewes F.C.|Lewes]]|clubnat=ENG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=17|pos=MF|name=[[Ashley Charles]]|age={{birth date and age|1999|05|15|df=yes}}|caps=8|goals=0|club=[[Bromley F.C.|Bromley]]|clubnat=ENG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=91|pos=MF|name=Romar Frank|age={{bda|df=y|1996|9|28}}|caps=34|goals=1|club=[[FC Camerhogne|Camerhogne]]|clubnat=GRN}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=6|pos=FW|name=Narshon Sylvester|age={{birth date and age|2007|01|15|df=yes}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[Hurricanes SC|Hurricanes]]|clubnat=GRN}}
{{nat fs g player|no=9|pos=FW|name=[[Lucas Akins]]|age={{birth date and age|1989|02|25|df=yes}}|caps=10|goals=2|club=[[Mansfield Town F.C.|Mansfield Town]]|clubnat=ENG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=15|pos=FW|name=[[Jermaine Francis]]|age={{bda|df=y|2002|3|15}}|caps=21|goals=3|club=[[Sutton United F.C.|Sutton United]]|clubnat=ENG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=18|pos=FW|name=Deanroy Phillip|age={{bda|df=y|2003|2|19}}|caps=10|goals=2|club=[[Otero College]]|clubnat=USA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=19|pos=FW|name=Vijay Valcin|age={{bda|df=y|2005|7|20}}|caps=10|goals=0|club=St. John's|clubnat=GRN}}
{{nat fs g player|no=20|pos=FW|name=Raheem Raeburn|age={{bda|df=y|2008|2|15}}|caps=5|goals=0|club=North Stars|clubnat=GRN}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Darius Johnson (footballer)|Darius Johnson]]|age={{bda|df=y|2000|3|15}}|caps=15|goals=2|club=[[San Jose Earthquakes]]|clubnat=USA}}
{{nat fs end}}

===Recent call-ups===
The following players have been called up within the last 12 months.

{{nat fs r start}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=GK|name=Chad Phillip|age={{bda|df=y|2000|8|9}}|caps=12|goals=0|club=[[FC Camerhogne|Camerhogne]]|clubnat=GRN|latest=v. {{fb|KEN}}, 30 March 2026}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Jeremy Richardson (footballer)|Jeremy Richardson]]|age={{bda|df=y|1998|03|03}}|caps=4|goals=0|club=[[Paradise FC International]]|clubnat=GRN|latest=v. {{fb|JAM}}, 18 January 2026}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Jason Belfon]]|age={{bda|df=y|1990|07|03}}|caps=52|goals=0|club=[[Paradise FC International]]|clubnat=GRN|latest=v. {{fb|SKN}}, 10 June 2025}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=5|pos=DF|name=Kimron Marshall|age={{bda|1993|11|05|df=y}}|caps=20|goals=1|club=[[FC Camerhogne|Camerhogne]]|clubnat=GRN|latest=v. {{fb|KEN}}, 30 March 2026}}
{{nat fs r player|no=17|pos=DF|name=Trevon Williams|age={{bda|1994|12|11|df=y}}|caps=17|goals=2|club=[[Queens Park Rangers SC|Queens Park Rangers]]|clubnat=GRN|latest=v. {{fb|KEN}}, 30 March 2026}}
{{nat fs r player|no=3|pos=DF|name=Jabari De Coteau|age={{birth date and age|2005|03|22|df=yes}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[Colorado Rapids]]|clubnat=USA|latest=v. {{fb|KEN}}, 30 March 2026}}
{{nat fs r player|no=4|pos=DF|name=[[Aaron Pierre (footballer)|Aaron Pierre]]|age={{bda|1993|2|17|df=y}}|caps=25|goals=0|club= [[Eastleigh F.C.|Eastleigh]] |clubnat=ENG|latest=v. {{fb|JAM}}, 18 January 2026}}
{{nat fs r player|no=5|pos=DF|name=Sawan Mark|age={{bda|2002|4|11|df=y}}|caps=4|goals=0|club=[[Hurricanes SC|Hurricanes]]|clubnat=GRN|latest=v. {{fb|JAM}}, 18 January 2026}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=Anthony Charles|age={{bda|2006|4|4|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Queens Park Rangers SC|Queens Park Rangers]]|clubnat=GRN|latest=v. {{fb|JAM}}, 18 January 2026}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=Johnathan Williams|age={{birth date and age|1994|12|30|df=yes}}|caps=1|goals=1|club=[[FC Camerhogne]]|clubnat=GRN|latest=v. {{fb|VIR}}, 14 November 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[AJ Paterson]]|age={{bda|1996|1|31|df=y}}|caps=20|goals=4|club= [[Birmingham Legion FC|Birmingham Legion]]|clubnat=USA|latest=v. {{fb|CUB}}, 11 October 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Roman Charles-Cook]]|age={{birth date and age|2003|12|22|df=yes}}|caps=5|goals=0|club=[[Dover Athletic F.C.|Dover Athletic]]|clubnat=ENG|latest=v. {{fb|CUB}}, 11 October 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=Ryhim Griffith|age={{bda|2001|5|12|df=y}}|caps=3|goals=0|club=[[Queens Park Rangers SC|Queens Park Rangers]]|clubnat=GRN|latest=v. {{fb|CUB}}, 11 October 2025}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=16|pos=MF|name=Keishon Clarke|age={{bda|df=y|2004|7|27}}|caps=7|goals=0|club=[[West Virginia University Institute of Technology#Athletics|West Virginia Tech]]|clubnat=USA|latest=v. {{fb|KEN}}, 30 March 2026}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=Corey Edwards|age={{birth date and age|2007|07|09|df=yes}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Stockport County F.C.|Stockport County]]|clubnat=ENG|latest=v. {{fb|KEN}}, 30 March 2026}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=Ethan Telesford|age={{bda|df=y|2003|11|13}}|caps=12|goals=0|club=[[FC Camerhogne]]|clubnat=GRN|latest=v. {{fb|JAM}}, 18 January 2026}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=E-Jay George|age={{bda|2007|2|23|df=y}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[FC Camerhogne]]|clubnat=GRN|latest=v. {{fb|VIR}}, 14 November 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=Hensen William|age=|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Paradise FC International|Paradise]]|clubnat=GRN|latest=v. {{fb|VIR}}, 14 November 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=Josh Thomas|age={{bda|df=y|2008|4|29}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[FC Camerhogne]]|clubnat=GRN|latest=v. {{fb|CUB}}, 11 October 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=Steffon Abraham|age={{bda|1999|12|29|df=y}}|caps=18|goals=0|club=[[Paradise FC International]]|clubnat=GRN|latest=v. {{fb|RUS}}, 19 March 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=Brady Charles|age={{bda|2001|11|5|df=y}}|caps=4|goals=0|club=[[Lincoln Blue Tigers|LU Blue Tigers]]|clubnat=USA|latest=v. {{fb|SKN}}, 10 June 2025}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=9|pos=FW|name=[[Joshua Isaac]]|age={{bda|2000|10|28|df=y}}|caps=18|goals=8|club=[[Paradise FC International]]|clubnat=GRN|latest=v. {{fb|KEN}}, 30 March 2026}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[D'Margio Wright-Phillips]]|age={{bda|df=y|2001|9|24}}|caps=1|goals=1|club=[[K Beerschot VA|Beerschot]]|clubnat=BEL|latest=v. {{fb|KEN}}, 30 March 2026}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=Ellis Lehane|age={{birth date and age|2006|10|02|df=yes}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Tottenham Hotspur F.C.|Tottenham Hotspur]]|clubnat=ENG|latest=v. {{fb|KEN}}, 30 March 2026}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=Zade Douglas|age={{bda|2006|7|28|df=y}}|caps=4|goals=0|club=[[Dodge City|Dodge City Conquistadors]]|clubnat=USA|latest=v. {{fb|JAM}}, 18 January 2026}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=Deondre Smith|age={{bda|df=y|2005|4|23}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Queens Park Rangers SC|Queens Park Rangers]]|clubnat=GRN|latest=v. {{fb|JAM}}, 18 January 2026}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Regan Charles-Cook]]|age={{bda|df=y|1997|2|14}}|caps=14|goals=3|club=[[Motherwell F.C.|Motherwell]]|clubnat=SCO|latest=v. {{fb|VIR}}, 14 November 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Keishean Francois]]|age={{bda|df=y|2003|8|17}}|caps=4|goals=2|club=North Surrey|clubnat=CAN|latest=v. {{fb|SKN}}, 10 June 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Saydrel Lewis]]|age={{bda|df=y|1997|11|27}}|caps=43|goals=9|club=SAB Spartans|clubnat=GRN|latest=v. {{fb|SKN}}, 10 June 2025}}
{{nat fs end}}

==Records==
{{Updated|14 November 2025}}<ref>{{cite web |last1=Mamrud |first1=Roberto |title=Grenada - Record International Players |url=https://www.rsssf.org/miscellaneous/grenada-recintlp.html |website=RSSSF |access-date=2 February 2023 |archive-date=17 December 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20221217165340/https://www.rsssf.org/miscellaneous/grenada-recintlp.html |url-status=live }}</ref>
:Players in '''bold''' still active with Grenada.

===Most appearances===
[[File:Cassim-langainge.jpg|200px|right|thumb|[[Cassim Langaigne]] is Grenada's most capped player with 72 appearances.]]

{| class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align:center;"
|-
!width=30px|Rank
! style="width:150px;"|Player
!width=50px|Caps
!width=50px|Goals
! style="width:100px;"|Period
|-
| 1
| style="text-align: left;"|[[Cassim Langaigne]]
| 72
| 6
| 2004–2016
|-
| 2
| style="text-align: left;"|[[Ricky Charles]]
| 71
| 37
| 1995–2011
|-
| 3
| style="text-align: left;"|[[Anthony Modeste (footballer, born 1975)|Anthony Modeste]]
| 67
| 13
| 1996–2011
|-
| 4
| style="text-align: left;"|'''[[Jason Belfon]]'''
| 55
| 0
| 2013–present
|-
| 5
| style="text-align: left;"|[[Patrick Modeste]]
| 53
| 6
| 1996–2015
|-
| rowspan=2|6
| style="text-align: left;"|[[Kithson Bain]]
| 49
| 17
| 2002–2015
|-
| style="text-align: left;"|[[Marc Marshall]]
| 49
| 0
| 2004–2015
|-
| 8
| style="text-align: left;"|'''[[Kwazim Theodore]]'''
| 45
| 1
| 2017–present
|-
| 9
| style="text-align: left;"|'''[[Shavon John-Brown]]'''
| 44
| 4
| 2012–present
|-
| 10
| style="text-align:left;"|'''[[Saydrel Lewis]]'''
| 43
| 9
| 2017–present
|-
|}

===Top goalscorers===
[[File:Ricky-charles.jpg|200px|right|thumb|[[Ricky Charles]] is Grenada's top scorer with 37 goals.]]

<!--Caps are not to be used as "tie-breakers". Sorted by ratio-->
{| class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align:center;"
|-
!width=30px|Rank
! style="width:150px;"|Player
!width=50px|Goals
!width=50px|Caps
!width=50px|Ratio
!width=100px|Period
|-
| 1
| style="text-align:left;"|[[Ricky Charles]]
| 37
| 71
| {{#expr:37/71 round 2}}
| 1995–2011
|-
| rowspan="2"|2
| style="text-align:left;"|'''[[Jamal Charles (footballer)|Jamal Charles]]'''
| 17
| 41
| {{#expr:17/41 round 2}}
| 2015–present
|-
| style="text-align:left;"|[[Kithson Bain]]
| 17
| 49
| {{#expr:17/49 round 2}}
| 2002–2015
|-
| 4
| style="text-align:left;"|[[Denis Rennie]]
| 14
| 28
| {{#expr:14/28 round 2}}
| 1999–2008
|-
| 5
| style="text-align: left;"|[[Anthony Modeste (footballer, born 1975)|Anthony Modeste]]
| 13
| 67
| {{#expr:13/67 round 2}}
| 1996–2011
|-
| 6
| style="text-align:left;"|'''[[Saydrel Lewis]]'''
| 9
| 43
| {{#expr:9/43 round 2}}
| 2017–present
|-
| 7
| style="text-align:left;"|'''[[Joshua Isaac]]'''
| 8
| 19
| {{#expr:8/19 round 2}}
| 2021–present
|-
| rowspan=2|8
| style="text-align:left;"|[[Keith Fletcher]]
| 7
| 6
| {{#expr:7/6 round 2}}
| 1990–2000
|-
| style="text-align:left;"|[[Jake Rennie]]
| 7
| 32
| {{#expr:7/32 round 2}}
| 2008–2017
|-
| rowspan=5|10
| style="text-align:left;"|'''[[Myles Hippolyte]]'''
| 6
| 10
| {{#expr:6/10 round 2}}
| 2023–present
|-
| style="text-align:left;"|[[Denron Frederick]]
| 6
| 19
| {{#expr:6/19 round 2}}
| 2009–2018
|-
| style="text-align:left;"|[[Clive Murray]]
| 6
| 20
| {{#expr:6/20 round 2}}
| 2011–2015
|-
| style="text-align:left;"|[[Patrick Modeste]]
| 6
| 53
| {{#expr:6/53 round 2}}
| 1996–2015
|-
| style="text-align:left;"|[[Cassim Langaigne]]
| 6
| 72
| {{#expr:6/72 round 2}}
| 2004–2016
|-
|}

==Competitive record==
===FIFA World Cup===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
!colspan=9|[[FIFA World Cup]]
!rowspan=19|
!colspan=6|[[FIFA World Cup qualification]]
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}*
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|[[1930 FIFA World Cup|1930]] to [[1978 FIFA World Cup|1978]]
|colspan=8 |''Not a FIFA member''
|colspan=6 |''Not a FIFA member''
|-
|{{flagicon|Spain}} [[1982 FIFA World Cup|1982]]
|colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|2||0||0||2||4||8
|-
|{{flagicon|Mexico}} [[1986 FIFA World Cup|1986]]
|colspan=8 | ''Withdrew''
|colspan=6 | ''Withdrew''
|-
|[[1990 FIFA World Cup|1990]] and [[1994 FIFA World Cup|1994]]
|colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|colspan=6|''Declined participation''
|-
|{{flagicon|France|1974}} [[1998 FIFA World Cup|1998]]
|colspan=8 rowspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|4||2||0||2||9||8
|-
|{{flagicon|South Korea|1997}} {{flagicon|Japan}} [[2002 FIFA World Cup|2002]]
|2||0||1||1||4||5
|-
|{{flagicon|Germany}} [[2006 FIFA World Cup|2006]]
|4||2||0||2||10||7
|-
|{{flagicon|South Africa}} [[2010 FIFA World Cup|2010]]
|3||1||1||1||12||5
|-
|{{flagicon|Brazil}} [[2014 FIFA World Cup|2014]]
|6||1||1||4||7||14
|-
|{{flagicon|Russia}} [[2018 FIFA World Cup|2018]]
|4||1||0||3||3||7
|-
|{{flagicon|Qatar}} [[2022 FIFA World Cup|2022]]
|4||1||0||3||2||5
|-
|{{flagicon|Canada}} {{flagicon|Mexico}} {{flagicon|United States}} [[2026 FIFA World Cup|2026]]
|4||2||1||1||11||7
|-
|{{flagicon|Morocco}} {{flagicon|Portugal}} {{flagicon|Spain}} [[2030 FIFA World Cup|2030]]
|colspan=8 rowspan=2|''To be determined''
|colspan=6 rowspan=2|''To be determined''
|-
|{{flagicon|Saudi Arabia}} [[2034 FIFA World Cup|2034]]
|-
!Total|| - ||{{Tooltip|0/11|Number of tournaments qualified for}}|| -|| - || - || - || - || - ||33||10||4||19||62||66
|}
:''*Draws include knockout matches decided via penalty shoot-out.''

===CONCACAF Championship & Gold Cup===
{{main|Grenada at the CONCACAF Gold Cup}}
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
!colspan=9|[[CONCACAF Championship]] & [[CONCACAF Gold Cup|Gold Cup]] record 
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{abbr|Pld|Games played}}
!{{abbr|W|Won}}
!{{abbr|D|Drawn}}*
!{{abbr|L|Lost}}
!{{abbr|GF|Goals for}}
!{{abbr|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|{{flagicon|SLV}} [[1963 CONCACAF Championship|1963]] to {{flagicon|HAI|1964}} [[1973 CONCACAF Championship|1973]]||colspan=8|''Part of {{flag|United Kingdom}}''
|-
|{{flagicon|MEX}} [[1977 CONCACAF Championship|1977]]||colspan=8|''Did not enter'' 
|-
|{{flagicon|HON|1949}} [[1981 CONCACAF Championship|1981]]||colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|-
|[[1985 CONCACAF Championship|1985]]||colspan=8|''Withdrew''
|-
|[[1989 CONCACAF Championship|1989]]||colspan=8 rowspan=2|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|United States}} [[1991 CONCACAF Gold Cup|1991]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Mexico}} {{flagicon|United States}} [[1993 CONCACAF Gold Cup|1993]] ||colspan=8 rowspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|United States}} [[1996 CONCACAF Gold Cup|1996]] 
|-
|{{flagicon|United States}} [[1998 CONCACAF Gold Cup|1998]]
|-
|{{flagicon|United States}} [[2000 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2000]]
|-
|{{flagicon|United States}} [[2002 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2002]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Mexico}} {{flagicon|United States}} [[2003 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2003]]
|-
|{{flagicon|United States}} [[2005 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2005]]
|-
|{{flagicon|United States}} [[2007 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2007]]
|-
|{{flagicon|United States}} [[2009 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2009]]||Group stage||12th||3||0||0||3||0||10
|-
|{{flagicon|United States}} [[2011 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2011]]||Group stage||11th||3||0||0||3||1||15
|-
|{{flagicon|United States}} [[2013 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2013]]||colspan=8 rowspan=4|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|Canada}} {{flagicon|United States}} [[2015 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2015]]
|-
|{{flagicon|United States}} [[2017 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2017]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Costa Rica}} {{flagicon|Jamaica}} {{flagicon|United States}} [[2019 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2019]]
|-
|{{flagicon|United States}} [[2021 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2021]]||Group stage||16th||3||0|||0||3||1||11
|-
|{{flagicon|Canada}} {{flagicon|United States}} [[2023 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2023]]||colspan=8 rowspan=2|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|Canada}} {{flagicon|United States}} [[2025 CONCACAF Gold Cup|2025]]
|-
!Total||Group stage||3/28||9||0||0||9||2||36
|}

===CONCACAF Nations League===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;"
! colspan="20" |[[CONCACAF Nations League]] record
|-
! colspan="10" |League
! rowspan="7" |
! colspan="9" |Finals
|-
!Season
!Division
!Group
! width="28" |{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
! width="28" |{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
! width="28" |{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
! width="28" |{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
! width="28" |{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
! width="28" |{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
! width="35" |{{Tooltip|P/R|Promotion/relegation at end of season}}
!'''Finals'''
!'''Result'''
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
!{{Tooltip|Squad|Finals Squad Only}}
|-
|[[2019–20 CONCACAF Nations League|2019–20]]
|[[2019–20 CONCACAF Nations League B|B]]
|[[2019–20 CONCACAF Nations League B#Group A|A]]
|6
|4
|2
|0
|8
|4
|{{rise}}
|{{flagicon|USA}} [[2021 CONCACAF Nations League Finals|2021]]
| colspan="8" |''Ineligible''
|-
|[[2022–23 CONCACAF Nations League|2022–23]]
|[[2022–23 CONCACAF Nations League A|A]]
|[[2022–23 CONCACAF Nations League A#Group D|D]]
|4
|0
|1
|3
|4
|17
|{{same position}}
|{{flagicon|USA}} [[2023 CONCACAF Nations League Finals|2023]]
| colspan="8" rowspan="2" |''Did not qualify''
|-
|[[2023–24 CONCACAF Nations League|2023–24]]
|[[2023–24 CONCACAF Nations League A|A]]
|[[2023–24 CONCACAF Nations League A#Group B|B]]
|4
|0
|1
|3
|2
|13
|{{down}}
|{{flagicon|USA}} [[2024 CONCACAF Nations League Finals|2024]]
|-
|[[2024–25 CONCACAF Nations League|2024–25]]
|[[2024–25 CONCACAF Nations League B|B]]
|[[2024–25 CONCACAF Nations League B#Group B|B]]
|6
|2
|1
|3
|7
|6
|{{same position}}
|{{flagicon|USA}} [[2025 CONCACAF Nations League Finals|2025]]
| colspan="8" |''Ineligible''
|-
!Total
!'''—'''
!'''—'''
!20
!6
!5
!9
!21
!40
!'''—'''
!'''Total'''
!'''0 Titles'''
!'''—'''
!'''—'''
!'''—'''
!'''—'''
!'''—'''
!'''—'''
!'''—'''
|}

===CFU Caribbean Cup===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center"
!colspan=9| [[CFU Championship]] & [[Caribbean Cup]] record
!rowspan=28|
!colspan=6| Qualification record
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{abbr|Pld|Games played}}
!{{abbr|W|Won}}
!{{abbr|D|Drawn}}*
!{{abbr|L|Lost}}
!{{abbr|GF|Goals for}}
!{{abbr|GA|Goals against}}
!Pld
!W
!D*
!L
!GF
!GA
|-
|{{flagicon|TRI}} [[1978 CFU Championship|1978]]||colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|colspan=6|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|SUR}} [[1979 CFU Championship|1979]]||colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
||2||0||0||2||1||3
|-
|{{flagicon|Puerto Rico}} [[1981 CFU Championship|1981]]||colspan=8 rowspan=2|''Did not enter''
|colspan=6 rowspan=2|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|French Guiana}} [[1983 CFU Championship|1983]]
|-
||{{flagicon|Barbados}} [[1985 CFU Championship|1985]]||colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|2||0||0||2||1||8
|-
|{{flagicon|Martinique|snake}} [[1988 CFU Championship|1988]]||colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|colspan=6|''Did not enter''
|-bgcolor=silver
|{{flagicon|Barbados}} [[1989 Caribbean Cup|1989]]||'''Runners-up'''||'''2nd'''||'''3'''||'''1'''||'''1'''||'''1'''||'''4'''||'''3'''
||4||4||0||0||13||1
|-
|{{flagicon|Trinidad and Tobago}} [[1990 Caribbean Cup|1990]]||Group Stage||5th||2||0||1||1||0||5
||3||1||2||0||4||2
|-
|{{flagicon|Jamaica}} [[1991 Caribbean Cup|1991]]||colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|colspan=6|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Trinidad and Tobago}} [[1992 Caribbean Cup|1992]]||colspan=8 rowspan=5|''Did not qualify''
||3||0||2||1||1||2
|-
|{{flagicon|Jamaica}} [[1993 Caribbean Cup|1993]]
||3||1||0||2||6||7
|-
|{{flagicon|Trinidad and Tobago}} [[1994 Caribbean Cup|1994]]
||2||1||0||1||4||4
|-
|{{flagicon|Cayman Islands}} {{flagicon|Jamaica}} [[1995 Caribbean Cup|1995]]
||2||1||0||1||2||4
|-
|{{flagicon|Trinidad and Tobago}} [[1996 Caribbean Cup|1996]]
||3||1||1||1||3||4
|-bgcolor=LemonChiffon
|{{flagicon|Antigua and Barbuda}} {{flagicon|St. Kitts and Nevis}} [[1997 Caribbean Cup|1997]]||'''Fourth place'''||'''4th'''||'''4'''||'''1'''||'''1'''||'''2'''||'''6'''||'''8'''
||2||1||1||0||6||2
|-
|{{flagicon|Trinidad and Tobago}} {{flagicon|Jamaica}} [[1998 Caribbean Cup|1998]]||colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
||3||2||0||1||17||4
|-
|{{flagicon|Trinidad and Tobago}} [[1999 Caribbean Cup|1999]]||Group stage||6th||3||1||0||2||3||10
||2||1||1||0||3||1
|-
|{{flagicon|Trinidad and Tobago}} [[2001 Caribbean Cup|2001]]||colspan=8 rowspan=3|''Did not qualify''
||3||1||0||2||6||7
|-
|{{flagicon|Barbados}} [[2005 Caribbean Cup|2005]]
||5||1||1||3||8||10
|-
|{{flagicon|Trinidad and Tobago}} [[2007 Caribbean Cup|2007]]
||3||0||1||2||1||3
|-bgcolor=silver
|{{flagicon|Jamaica}} [[2008 Caribbean Cup|2008]]||'''Runners-up'''||'''2nd'''||'''5'''||'''2'''||'''1'''||'''2'''||'''8'''||'''11'''
||5||2||1||2||10||9
|-bgcolor=LemonChiffon
|{{flagicon|Martinique|snake}} [[2010 Caribbean Cup|2010]]||'''Fourth place'''||'''4th'''||'''5'''||'''1'''||'''2'''||'''2'''||'''3'''||'''4'''
||3||2||0||1||5||4
|-
|{{flagicon|Antigua and Barbuda}} [[2012 Caribbean Cup|2012]]||colspan=8 rowspan=3|''Did not qualify''
||3||1||1||1||3||4
|-
|{{flagicon|Jamaica}} [[2014 Caribbean Cup|2014]]
||3||0||2||1||4||5
|-
|{{flagicon|Martinique|snake}} [[2017 Caribbean Cup|2017]]
||4||2||0||2||11||9
|-
!Total
!Runners-up||2nd||22||6||6||10||24||41
!60||22||13||25||109||93
|}

==Honours==
===Regional===
* '''[[Caribbean Cup]]'''
** {{silver2}} Runners-up (2): [[1989 Caribbean Cup|1989]], [[2008 Caribbean Cup|2008]]

==References==
{{Reflist}}

==External links==
*[https://web.archive.org/web/20070609081712/http://www.fifa.com/associations/association=grn/index.html Grenada] at FIFA.com (archived 9 June 2007)
*[https://web.archive.org/web/20131216043338/http://www.concacaf.com/team/grenada Grenada] at CONCACAF.com (archived 16 December 2013)
*[http://www.grenadafa.com/ Grenada Football Association] (official website)

{{Grenada national football team}}
{{Navboxes
|title=Grenada squads – CONCACAF Gold Cup
|bg=#FCD116
|fg=#007A5E
|bordercolor=#CE1126
|list1=
{{Grenada squad 2009 CONCACAF Gold Cup}}
{{Grenada squad 2011 CONCACAF Gold Cup}}
{{Grenada squad 2021 CONCACAF Gold Cup}}
}}
{{Football in Grenada}}
{{CONCACAF teams}}
{{National sports teams of Grenada}}

{{DEFAULTSORT:Grenada National Football Team}}
[[Category:Grenada national football team| ]]
[[Category:Caribbean national association football teams]]
